author: @sierraecho884
text: 3D printing came so far. For 400 bucks you get a Core XY printer with almost zero fumbling around. Just load a file push a button and off you go. Print speeds and quality are amazing too.

author: @BrianMeeks
text:
I decided to buy my first 3D printer (Mingda Magician Pro 3D) because I became enthralled with your videos.

It arrived a week ago and I've still not taken it out of the box. Not because I don't want to or am afraid, but because I'm cleaning out a room in the basement, building a table for it, and making it my 3D printing sanctuary.

My excitement for learning all about this is through the roof.

Thanks so much for all the wonderful videos you make.

author: @trevorhaakenson8459
text: Nice! You are making some good information videos now with good editing. Good job!

author: @AndreaDingbatt
text:
SANTAAAA!! I've been a very good girl,,, Please, please please can I have a 3D Printer so I can help the Elves make Toys for Children of All Ages!!
Lol!! Thank you Robert, much appreciated the work you put in to the video!!❤

text:
"If it melts, we can print it."
Arnold Schwarzenegger, Predator.

author: @robertsmith4681
text: One thing I haven't quite figured out how too do yet is print assembled mechanical pieces such as sealed clockwork or gear mechanisms, I can never get the slicer to leave required gaps between surface so things actually work when they are finished printing.

author: @ez1913
text:
Intriguing is the independent dual extruder and conductive filaments.
Print the conductor cable inside the framework or rotor.

author: @paulbrancato8262
text: Saw a video yesterday of a Australian company that's 3d printing glass. I think it's name is Maple Glass Printing. Interesting concept but I'm not sure where they are going with it. The scrap glass has to be processed into rods before being fed to the printer.

author: @brentedwards2606
text: Is there any way to contact you directly? I have an idea about something in your past videos that I would like to collaborate on.
